speaker: alex
message: Hey! Welcome to the backend team. I see you've set up the Express app — nice clean structure. Let's start with input validation on the users endpoint.

speaker: user
message: Sounds good, I'll use zod for the validation.

speaker: alex
message: Just approved your validation PR. Nice work! One suggestion: consider extracting shared validators into a common module for reuse.

speaker: user
message: Good idea, I'll add that as a follow-up.

speaker: jordan
message: I'm reviewing your auth middleware tests. Overall solid but you're missing a case for malformed tokens — not just missing or expired, but actually garbage input.

speaker: user
message: Good catch, I'll add that test case.

speaker: jordan
message: Approved. Good improvement on the test coverage.

speaker: alex
message: Ready for something more challenging? The database connection pooling task is up next. It builds on the work you did with the users endpoint.

speaker: user
message: Let's do it. Any tips on pool sizing?

speaker: alex
message: Start with pg-pool docs. Key tradeoff: more connections means more concurrent queries but more memory. For our use case, 10 connections in prod is a good starting point.

speaker: sam
message: Hey! I'm Sam from the frontend team. When you're ready to build the UI for the user list, let me know — I can help with the component structure.

speaker: jordan
message: Before you move to frontend, make sure the db pooling PR is solid. I'll be reviewing it closely.
